invariants.scope: validation-rows.json ledger and all artifacts derived from it
invariants.rule: validation-rows.json is the sole authoritative store of row state; system_validation.json is a write-only projection produced exclusively by emit-system-validation; judgments are never hand-transcribed
invariants.enforcement: emit-system-validation is the only producer of the gate artifact; Phase 8 doc forbids hand-editing; self-check runs on exact emitted bytes
invariants.verification: pytest: mutate ledger, emit, assert projection matches ledger judged rows exactly; assert hand-built artifact fails self-check provenance rules

invariants.rule: Only parse-reply, assemble-digest, and cancel-batch mutate the ledger; each holds validation-rows.json.lock for the full read-modify-write and writes atomic tmp+rename; all other subcommands are read-only
invariants.enforcement: module structure: single mutation helper requiring the lock; read-only subcommands have no write path
invariants.verification: pytest: run every read-only subcommand against a ledger fixture, assert file bytes unchanged; assert mutating subcommands fail with exit 3 LEDGER_BUSY when lock is held

invariants.scope: subcommand exit contract and reply atomicity
invariants.rule: Every subcommand exits 0, 2 (structured {code,row_id,detail} issues on stdout), or 3; an invalid or partially valid reply produces zero ledger mutations and a re-prompt quoting the offending text
invariants.enforcement: CLI boundary maps exceptions to exit codes; parse-reply validates the full reply before any mutation
invariants.verification: pytest: feed reply with one valid and one invalid block, assert ledger bytes unchanged and re-prompt names offending text (TC-3.2); assert exit-code mapping for ok/validation/lock/corrupt paths

invariants.scope: inbound judgment trust boundary (parse-reply) and allowlist sourcing
invariants.rule: A telegram-sourced judgment is applied only when --sender-id matches the project's registry-configured allowlist (never hardcoded); non-allowlisted replies are discarded and logged, never parsed into the ledger
invariants.enforcement: parse-reply requires --sender-id for --source telegram; allowlist loaded from telegram registry config
exceptions: --source terminal asserts local operator is Jason (single-user machine, documented)
invariants.verification: pytest: non-allowlisted sender with valid reply text, assert ledger unchanged and discard logged; allowlisted sender, assert applied; assert no allowlist literal in module source (static grep)

invariants.scope: self-check gate parity and oracle lint
invariants.rule: self-check mirrors every gate reject class, is strictly stricter than the gate (identical-set anti-relabeling extension), and must pass on the exact bytes passed to mark_system_validation_complete immediately before every call
invariants.enforcement: Phase 8 close sequence orders self-check directly before the MCP call; parity pinned by test against the served-code contract extract
invariants.verification: pytest parity suite: for each gate reject class in fizzy-validation-contract.md, construct a fixture that the gate would reject and assert self-check rejects with the mirrored code (TC-3.3); identical-set fixture rejected locally

invariants.scope: digest batch lifecycle and Phase 8 close idempotency
invariants.rule: At most one digest batch is open at any time; batches snapshot row hashes at open; replies referencing a non-active digest-id or changed row hash are rejected; close re-entry at any step skips completed work without re-judging
invariants.enforcement: assemble-digest refuses while a batch is open; parse-reply validates digest-id and snapshot hashes; close sequence checks NOTHING_TO_DIGEST, existing clean emission, and card metadata before the MCP call
exceptions: cancel-batch closes an open batch without judgments (audit-logged)
invariants.verification: pytest: second assemble-digest while open fails; stale digest-id reply rejected; row-hash-changed reply rejected; double close-step run produces identical end state (idempotency)

invariants.scope: judgment provenance and append-only audit history
invariants.rule: Every non-null result carries a full provenance block (digest_id, source, reply_ref, judged_at); judgment_history is append-only; supersessions and batch cancellations carry audit fields including approval_ref; no history entry is ever rewritten
invariants.enforcement: emit-system-validation refuses results without provenance; reset moves judgments into history rather than erasing
invariants.verification: pytest: emit refuses provenance-less result; --reset-failed preserves original fail in history; supersession without approval_ref rejected by check-rows
